annotations.label: Self-adjoint operator

anchor.snippet: that is its own adjoint

annotations.note: Mathlib's `IsSelfAdjoint x ↔ star x = x` captures "its own adjoint" in any star algebra, specializing to operators via the adjoint.

annotations.label: Finite-dimensional spectral theorem

anchor.snippet: By the finite-dimensional spectral theorem

annotations.note: A self-adjoint operator on a finite-dimensional inner product space is diagonalized by an orthonormal eigenbasis.

annotations.label: Hamiltonian operator

anchor.snippet: Of particular significance is the Hamiltonian operator

annotations.note: The quantum-mechanical Hamiltonian operator is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Self-adjoint iff unitarily equivalent to multiplication

anchor.snippet: they are unitarily equivalent to real-valued multiplication operators

annotations.note: The multiplication-operator form of the spectral theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graph and extension of an operator

anchor.snippet: The graph of an (arbitrary) operator

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the graph of a (partial) linear operator and an extension order `≤` on `LinearPMap`s.

annotations.label: Adjoint operator

anchor.snippet: acts on the subspace

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the adjoint of a densely-defined unbounded operator acting on its adjoint domain.

annotations.label: Symmetric (Hermitian) operator

anchor.snippet: is called symmetric (or Hermitian ) if

annotations.note: `LinearMap.IsSymmetric T` is defined as `⟪T x, y⟫ = ⟪x, T y⟫`, the symmetric/Hermitian condition.

annotations.label: Symmetric operators are closable

anchor.snippet: symmetric operators are always closable

annotations.note: Closability of unbounded operators is defined, but the theorem that symmetric operators are closable was not found.

annotations.label: Self-adjoint operator

anchor.snippet: is called self-adjoint if

annotations.note: `LinearPMap.IsSelfAdjoint A ↔ A† = A` is the self-adjointness of a densely-defined unbounded operator.

annotations.label: Essentially self-adjoint operator

anchor.snippet: is said to be essentially self-adjoint if the closure of

annotations.note: The operator closure and self-adjointness exist, but "essentially self-adjoint" is not defined as a named notion.

annotations.label: Hellinger–Toeplitz theorem

anchor.snippet: According to Hellinger–Toeplitz theorem

annotations.note: A symmetric operator on a complete space is automatically continuous, the Hellinger–Toeplitz theorem.

annotations.label: Bounded self-adjoint operator

anchor.snippet: is self-adjoint if

annotations.note: For a bounded operator, `IsSelfAdjoint` is equivalent to the symmetric inner-product condition.

annotations.label: Complex form of a bounded operator

anchor.snippet: can be written in the complex form

annotations.note: `a = ℜa + I • ℑa` with `ℜa, ℑa` self-adjoint is exactly the Cartesian/complex decomposition T = A + iB.

annotations.label: Positive bounded operators are self-adjoint

anchor.snippet: every positive bounded linear operator

annotations.note: A positive bounded operator is self-adjoint; over ℂ, `isPositive_iff_complex` shows nonneg inner products suffice.

annotations.label: Invertibility criterion

anchor.snippet: is invertible if the image of

annotations.note: The criterion that a self-adjoint operator with dense, bounded-below range is invertible was not found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Operator norm formula

anchor.snippet: The operator norm is given by

annotations.note: The operator norm of a symmetric operator equals the supremum of the absolute Rayleigh quotient.

annotations.label: Eigenvalues real, eigenvectors orthogonal

anchor.snippet: the eigenvalues are real and the corresponding eigenvectors are orthogonal

annotations.note: Eigenvalues are real (`conj_eigenvalue_eq_self`) and eigenspaces orthogonal (`orthogonalFamily_eigenspaces`).

annotations.label: Compact self-adjoint operators have an eigenvalue

anchor.snippet: Bounded self-adjoint operators do not necessarily have an eigenvalue

annotations.note: A nonzero compact symmetric operator must have a nonzero eigenvalue, with eigenspaces spanning the space.

annotations.label: Resolvent set

anchor.snippet: The resolvent set (or regular set ) of

annotations.note: The resolvent set is defined for any algebra element; bounded operators are a special case.

annotations.label: Spectrum

anchor.snippet: is defined as the complement

annotations.note: `spectrum R a` is defined as the complement of the resolvent set, for general algebra elements.

annotations.label: Spectrum of self-adjoint operator is real

anchor.snippet: The spectrum of a self-adjoint operator is always real

annotations.note: The spectrum of a self-adjoint C*-algebra element is real; this covers bounded operators but not the unbounded case.

annotations.label: Bounded normal: real spectrum iff self-adjoint

anchor.snippet: the spectrum is real if and only if the operator is self-adjoint

annotations.note: A normal element with real spectrum is self-adjoint, and conversely (`IsSelfAdjoint.val_re_map_spectrum`).

annotations.label: Bounded below

anchor.snippet: is called bounded below

annotations.note: A named "bounded below" predicate for operators in this sense was not found.

annotations.label: Self-adjoint operator has real spectrum
annotations.note: Mathlib proves real spectrum for bounded self-adjoint (C*-algebra) elements, not for the unbounded operators of this theorem box.

annotations.label: Symmetric operator with real spectrum is self-adjoint

anchor.value: Symmetric operator with real spectrum is self-adjoint

annotations.note: Mathlib has the bounded normal-element version; the unbounded symmetric-operator statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Spectral theorem (informal)

anchor.snippet: a self-adjoint operator has an orthonormal basis of eigenvectors

annotations.note: An orthonormal eigenbasis is provided in finite dimensions (and eigenspaces are dense for compact operators).

annotations.label: Multiplication operator

anchor.section: Multiplication operator form of the spectral theorem
anchor.snippet: is called a multiplication operator

annotations.note: The multiplication operator on an L² space is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Multiplication operators are self-adjoint

anchor.section: Multiplication operator form of the spectral theorem
anchor.snippet: Any multiplication operator is a self-adjoint operator

annotations.note: No multiplication operator construction exists, so its self-adjointness is not formalized.

annotations.label: Unitary equivalence of operators

anchor.section: Multiplication operator form of the spectral theorem
anchor.snippet: are unitarily equivalent if and only if there is a unitary transformation

annotations.note: While `unitary` elements exist, the relation of unitary equivalence between operators is not defined.

annotations.label: Unitary equivalence preserves boundedness/self-adjointness

anchor.section: Multiplication operator form of the spectral theorem
anchor.snippet: If unitarily equivalent

annotations.note: Preservation of self-adjointness under unitary equivalence of operators is not formalized.

annotations.label: Spectral theorem for bounded and unbounded operators

anchor.section: Multiplication operator form of the spectral theorem
anchor.snippet: The spectral theorem holds for both bounded and unbounded self-adjoint operators

annotations.note: The multiplication-operator spectral theorem for general self-adjoint operators is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spectrum equals essential range

anchor.section: Multiplication operator form of the spectral theorem
anchor.snippet: is just the essential range of

annotations.note: The identification of the spectrum with the essential range of a multiplier is not formalized.

annotations.label: Self-adjoint operator is unitarily equivalent to a multiplication operator

anchor.value: is unitarily equivalent to a multiplication operator

annotations.note: The multiplication-operator form of the spectral theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Functional calculus

anchor.snippet: to define a functional calculus

annotations.note: Mathlib has a continuous functional calculus `cfc` for bounded self-adjoint elements; the Borel/unbounded calculus is not formalized.

annotations.note: The time-evolution functional calculus exp(itH) of a quantum Hamiltonian is not formalized.

annotations.note: Projection-valued measures / resolutions of the identity are not formalized in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: the following Stieltjes integral representation for T can be proved

annotations.note: The spectral Stieltjes integral representation of a self-adjoint operator is not formalized.

annotations.note: Dirac bra-ket formulations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.note: The physics "resolution of unity" (completeness relation) is not formalized.

annotations.note: Compactness of the inverse of this specific operator is not formalized.

annotations.label: Adjoint is not symmetric

annotations.label: Stone's theorem

anchor.snippet: self-adjoint operators are precisely the infinitesimal generators of unitary groups

annotations.note: Stone's theorem on one-parameter unitary groups is not formalized (only Stone–Weierstrass exists).

annotations.label: Schrödinger operator essential self-adjointness range

anchor.snippet: is essentially self-adjoint for 0 < α ≤ 2 but not for α > 2

annotations.note: This essential self-adjointness range result for Schrödinger operators is not formalized.

annotations.label: No self-adjoint momentum operator on a half-line

anchor.snippet: There is no self-adjoint momentum operator

annotations.note: Nonexistence of a self-adjoint momentum operator on a half-line is not formalized.
